metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = converging / accurate region, CRIMSON = diverging / error blowup
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no complex-analysis proof of zero radius of convergence, no Borel resummation, no QED fine-structure application, no Bender-Wu coefficients formula
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B19: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. A student runs ten perturbative corrections to a quartic oscillator. Each term is smaller than the last. Looks like convergence.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. A student runs ten perturbative corrections to a quartic oscillator. Each term is smaller than the last. Looks like convergence.

beats.narration_text: Term eleven. Instead of shrinking again, the correction explodes — and keeps exploding. Everything past term ten is worse than term ten.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Term eleven. Instead of shrinking again, the correction explodes — and keeps exploding. Everything past term ten is worse than term ten.

beats.narration_text: A series whose terms keep shrinking should converge. Here every term shrank for ten orders — then the whole thing blew up. Why?

props.headline: A series whose terms keep shrinking should converge. Here every term…

card.headline: A series that shrinks should converge.
card.dek: Here every term shrank for ten orders — then blew up. Why?

beats.narration_text: Perturbation theory expands the energy as a power series in a coupling constant. Smaller coupling means smaller terms — that's the whole promise.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Perturbation theory expands the energy as a power series in a coupling constant. Smaller coupling means smaller terms — that's the whole promise.

beats.narration_text: If ten terms all shrink, a mathematician would say: evidence of convergence. Keep computing more terms and the answer improves forever.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: If ten terms all shrink, a mathematician would say: evidence of convergence. Keep computing more terms and the answer improves forever.

beats.narration_text: A series converges when its partial sums close in on a fixed target. The terms shrinking is a necessary sign — but not sufficient proof.

props.headline: A series converges when its partial sums close in on a…

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A series converges when its partial sums close in on a fixed target. The terms shrinking is a necessary sign — but not sufficient proof.

beats.narration_text: Here's the catch. Flip the coupling negative — make the quartic term minus lambda instead of plus. The potential now slopes down to negative infinity.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Here's the catch. Flip the coupling negative — make the quartic term minus lambda instead of plus. The potential now slopes down to negative infinity.

beats.narration_text: For any negative lambda, however small, there is no ground state. The particle escapes to infinity. The system simply does not exist.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: For any negative lambda, however small, there is no ground state. The particle escapes to infinity. The system simply does not exist.

beats.narration_text: That means the energy has a singularity on the negative coupling axis. Any Taylor series centered at zero can't reach that singularity — its radius of convergence is zero.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: That means the energy has a singularity on the negative coupling axis. Any Taylor series centered at zero can't reach that singularity — its radius of convergen

beats.narration_text: The series coefficients grow like a factorial — k-factorial. That factorial growth eventually overtakes any fixed power of lambda, no matter how small.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The series coefficients grow like a factorial — k-factorial. That factorial growth eventually overtakes any fixed power of lambda, no matter how small.

beats.narration_text: So the terms first shrink because lambda-to-the-k gets small, then blow up because k-factorial gets huge. There is a crossover — and that crossover is the key.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: So the terms first shrink because lambda-to-the-k gets small, then blow up because k-factorial gets huge. There is a crossover — and that crossover is the key.

beats.narration_text: Plot the cumulative error against truncation order. It descends to a minimum at N-star — the optimal truncation — then climbs steeply. Stop at N-star.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Plot the cumulative error against truncation order. It descends to a minimum at N-star — the optimal truncation — then climbs steeply. Stop at N-star.

beats.narration_text: At N-star the error is exponentially small — of order e to the minus constant over lambda. Invisible to every finite-order expansion, but real.

props.headline: At N-star the error is exponentially small — of order e…

beats.narration_text: Dyson made this argument in 1952 for quantum electrodynamics. Flip the sign of the fine-structure constant and the vacuum destabilizes. The QED series also has zero radius of convergence.

props.headline: Dyson made this argument in 1952 for quantum electrodynamics. Flip the…

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Dyson made this argument in 1952 for quantum electrodynamics. Flip the sign of the fine-structure constant and the vacuum destabilizes. The QED series also has

beats.narration_text: Yet QED is the most accurate physical theory ever tested. Its perturbative predictions match experiment to twelve decimal places — because they stop at optimal order.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Yet QED is the most accurate physical theory ever tested. Its perturbative predictions match experiment to twelve decimal places — because they stop at optimal

beats.narration_text: Illustrative: a student computes eight corrections for coupling 0.05. The terms read 0.82, 0.94, 0.975, 0.990, 0.996, 0.998, 0.9985, 0.9986. Then term nine: 1.002. Ten: 1.015. Eleven: 1.07. Twelve: 1.4. Optimal truncation was term eight.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Illustrative: a student computes eight corrections for coupling 0.05. The terms read 0.82, 0.94, 0.975, 0.990, 0.996, 0.998, 0.9985, 0.9986. Then term nine: 1.0

beats.narration_text: A divergent series can give the right answer — if you stop at the optimal term. Quantum mechanics.

props.headline: A divergent series can give the right answer — if you…

card.headline: Stop at the optimal term.
card.dek: A divergent series gives the right answer — if you know when to stop.
